nakshatras.whatIs: Ashwini (Aśvinī) is one of the 27 nakshatras (lunar mansions) of Vedic astrology. In the Vimshottari dasha system recorded in the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra, Ashwini is ruled by Ketu (South Node). For those born under this nakshatra (Janma Nakshatra), the traditional remedial practice is the mantra of its ruling planet — the Vedic name mantra "Om Ketave Namah" or the tantric seed mantra "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah". Strengthening the nakshatra lord is held to support the significations of one's birth star. Note: pairing a nakshatra with its lord's mantra follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial application is traditional convention.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ केतवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Ketave Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Ketu (Vimshottari lord of Ashwini, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Ketu (South Node), Vimshottari lord of Ashwini nakshatra.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ स्रां स्रीं स्रौं सः केतवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Ketu bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Ketu (South Node), the lord of Ashwini, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Ketu
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Ashwini nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Ashwini is ruled by Ketu (South Node) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Ketave Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Ashwini nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Ketu (South Node) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Ashwini nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

In the Vimshottari dasha system recorded in the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra, Purva Bhadrapada is ruled by Guru (Jupiter). For those born under this nakshatra (Janma Nakshatra), the traditional remedial practice is the mantra of its ruling planet — the Vedic name mantra "Om Gurave Namah" or the tantric seed mantra "Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah". Strengthening the nakshatra lord is held to support the significations of one's birth star. Note: pairing a nakshatra with its lord's mantra follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial application is traditional convention.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ गुरवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Gurave Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Guru (Vimshottari lord of Purva Bhadrapada, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Guru (Jupiter), Vimshottari lord of Purva Bhadrapada nakshatra.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ ग्रां ग्रीं ग्रौं सः गुरवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Guru bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Guru (Jupiter), the lord of Purva Bhadrapada, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Guru
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

nakshatras.whatIs: Revati (Revatī) is one of the 27 nakshatras (lunar mansions) of Vedic astrology.
